paragraphs.body: Can you imagine a person swearing at guests as they come in? Or someone takes the cursing word right out of your mouth, but the people you hate can do nothing to you. Well, maybe there is no such human being who can do this for you, but an adorable lively creature can - cockatiel. People always need a companion, but sometimes it’s hard to find. As humans, we need not only physical health but mental support by talking to others or just simply complaining about how our life sucks. A cockatiel, an intelligent parrot with two blushes on their face, can be so majestic that it acts like you had a kid without giving any birth to a child. Maybe you think birds are annoying or pooping a lot, yes, they do, and from my personal experience, I can certainly tell you, I hated these birds before I got my first cockatiel named Color.

annotations.text: Can you imagine a person swearing at guests as they come in?

paragraphs.body: If you are still reading, that’s awesome. Now, before I get into my irritating life with my cockatiel, I would love to show you a peek at, “Why Cockatiels Are The Best Pets?”, because I just want to make sure in case my writing is so bad that it makes you think they are the worst pet in the world, I hope at least this essay can hold your thoughts on how cockatiels can be good sometimes. In the essay, author Sani Hussain suggests cockatiels are the best pets, not only because they are adorable, but also very intelligent (not all of them are). He argues his point by developing several important ideas. First, Hussain claims cockatiels can mimic humans and do many things that humans can do, such as playing mini basketball. Sometimes they know how to kiss, dance, and speak like humans do. Next, he portrays the cockatiel as a very affectionate pet who can stay by your side for a long time. When you are busy working on things, they can sit next to you or on your shoulder preening themselves. Even though they want your attention, which can be a little annoying sometimes, they give you unconditional love no matter what’s happening (except when you bully them). Third, Hussain demonstrates their poops are small and easy to clean by comparing them to dogs and cats’ poops. He also implies that it’s very simple to take care of them by feeding them on time and cleaning their cage. Finally, the author suggests not all cockatiels are smart, but if you put a lot of time and effort into it, they can become extremely clever.

annotations.text: In the essay, author Sani Hussain suggests cockatiels are the best pets, not only because they are adorable, but also very intelligent (not all of them are).

If you are still reading, that’s awesome. Now, before I get into my irritating life with my cockatiel, I would love to show you a peek at, “Why Cockatiels Are The Best Pets?”, because I just want to make sure in case my writing is so bad that it makes you think they are the worst pet in the world, I hope at least this essay can hold your thoughts on how cockatiels can be good sometimes.

paragraphs.body: Clever, such a great word describes the complete opposite of Color. During the year of COVID-19, my sister and I got so bored that we started begging my mom to buy us a pet to play with. Unfortunately, my mom is a zoophobe, who hates animals especially when they are furry. She believes animals are dirty, scary, and have a high possibility of hurting people, so you might need to get a rabies vaccine. She also hates cleaning up poop when she already has four kids to take care of. One of her most famous, or maybe universal, quotes is that: “You couldn't feed yourself, let alone the pets.” But her words did not discourage us, instead, we kept begging her day and night saying that we could use our own money. In the end, we had an agreement - buy a pet that’s not furry and easy to be taken care of. I wasn't that upset compared to my sister, because she wants a dog or cat so bad, while I know my mom is giving up her bottom line. And also, because I love cockatoos (by watching YouTube) - a large really intelligent parrot - which all matched with the agreement description, and more importantly, they poop once every couple hours, except the fact that they are very expensive. As a result, we bought a cockatiel, because the staff told us they are the best pets to have as a beginner. However, I hate cockatiels because I think they are very unattractive and ugly like a malnourished chick, and especially their nose and head feathers look so weird. They literally look like bananas, and act like a fake cockatoo. Everyone was saying that they are so cute, but I think people are just exaggerating because all I see is that they are eerie and ugly as xxxx (bad word). I still remember one thing that’s vivid in my mind is that I said to my sister with such confidence: “The only bird I will never buy is a cockatiel.” However, thinking she is not cute is not a deal anymore because the only choice is she, I mean, at least it’s something. After buying her home, we started with enthusiasm thinking we were 100 percent able to take care of her well. But as time passed, I noticed that the only thing my bird knows how to do every day is pooping a lot everywhere. For some reason, she loves to poop on me (head and shoulder, even on my homework!!! And eat my homework too, meanwhile, you don’t get an excuse from “my bird poop or ate my homework”). I get very mad when she does that to me, but I can't do anything to her. Sometimes my sister slaps her on her back (no worries, it's very soft) to show her anger, but this stupid bird has no idea we are mad. To solve the Pooping Everywhere Problem, I even tried to train her on potty-pooping (too stupid to understand what I try to teach her) and bought bird diapers. Instead of being very appreciative of all my efforts, she looked like Emotional Damage when I put the diaper on her; therefore, I stopped using it. Now I have to suffer from all the poops she made every half an hour.

annotations.text: Unfortunately, my mom is a zoophobe, who hates animals especially when they are furry.
she looked like Emotional Damage when I put the diaper on her; therefore, I stopped using it.
annotations.comment: This essay is so fun to read. It's hilarious :)

annotations.text: However, I hate cockatiels because I think they are very unattractive and ugly like a malnourished chick,

paragraphs.body: As time passed, we gradually accepted the fact that she was what she was, even though she kept making the mess. Cleaning her cage and spending time with her had become my daily routine. She became so affectionate and wanted my attention through our interaction. As she joined my family, I realized that I had changed my opinion on cockatiels and that I was willing to take care of this bird despite its problems. Without a doubt, love can be about service, about giving than it is about receiving. Sometimes when I sit down with Color on my thigh, I flashback to when my mom described me when I was little. Did I also poop a lot? Or being so ignorant that maybe she also wants to slap me on my back. I know she said I hated eating when I was a kid, so three people were chasing after me trying to get me to eat: my godmother, grand-godmother, and my grand-godfather. From kitchen to bedroom, from bedroom to the yard.

annotations.text: Without a doubt, love can be about service, about giving than it is about receiving. Sometimes when I sit down with Color on my thigh, I flashback to when my mom described me when I was little. Did I also poop a lot?
annotations.comment: Aha! Lots of friction, heat, and energy in this section. Let's talk about this idea as a "center of gravity" for your essay as a whole.

paragraphs.body: They must be so cooked with me that I did it every day, such as stealing their high heels to wear, arguing over a toy, or just crying a lot.  Even though I wasn't counted as their real blood-related child, they treated me like I was their true kid, treated me like I was their little angel. But they never complain (maybe joking about how I was such a bad kid). Exhausted, but kept staying by my side, and put me into my sweet dream. In the essay, “Why Cockatiels Are The Best Pets?”, Hussian also writes, “Even when you go, as soon as you come back they will be waiting for you. Some cockatiels can also be free roam, and will stay by your side for long periods of time”(Hussain 1). In other words, Hussian is saying Cockatiel will always be there for you, just like they are always here to support me, no matter what I did wrong. Like cockatiels, they gave me unconditional love. On the other hand, I’m just like a little cockatiel to them, annoying and troublesome, but there is an invisible bond between us, that they loved me, no matter what I did, just as I got obsessed with my infuriating cockatiel.

annotations.text: On the other hand, I’m just like a little cockatiel to them, annoying and troublesome, but there is an invisible bond between us, that they loved me, no matter what I did,

paragraphs.body: Many people argue that the bloodline is more important than all the efforts and emotions you put into a person. I disagree because humans are like cockatiels just as Hussain said, “Not least cockatiels are intelligent birds. If owners train them from the beginning or put effort in them they can be very loving”(Hussain 1). In his essay, Hussain believes the time you spend on something is way more important, and it acts like a magical potion that just changes things from hate to love. I gave so much of my time and attention to Color that now I think cockatiels are so cute and adorable. Their nose and head feathers are no longer weird to me. And the fact that they look like bananas also somehow changed, they do look like bananas tho, but a cute banana that is so tiny-tiny adorable makes me wanna cry and kiss them so hard. And their smells too, so milky that I wanna eat them within one bite. For those who like babies, the babies are cute maybe because they are stupid, and to me, my birds are cute maybe because she is also very stupid. But I loved her, just like my family loves me even when I’m brainless that I’m easily scammed. Even though they are not here next to me, our love and feelings are nevertheless, passing through time and space, and bonded strongly between us.

annotations.text: Many people argue that the bloodline is more important than all the efforts and emotions you put into a person.
annotations.comment: Interesting! Can you choose your own "family" in life? What does it mean to be "family"?

paragraphs.body: They are like a cockatiel to me, and I’m like a little cockatiel to them. That’s all what this is about.

annotations.text: They are like a cockatiel to me, and I’m like a little cockatiel to them.
